title: galium obliquum var. alpicola
intro: Galium obliquum var. alpicola, also known as alpine bedstraw, is a variety of the Galium obliquum plant, a type of flowering plant in the bedstraw family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. The herbal preparations of alpine bedstraw typically include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
